pi_items.source: verifier.py:61 (network checks expect 'frontend' and 'backend' bridge networks in the result JSON)
verifier.py:154 (web-proxy is passed to check_container and referenced again for the isolation bonus)
verifier.py:154 (web-proxy check_container call ensures the image string contains 'nginx')
verifier.py:154 (check_container is invoked for web-proxy with ['frontend'] as the expected networks)
verifier.py:154 (web-proxy check_container call only flags ['backend'] as forbidden, so 'bridge' is not enforced)
verifier.py:160 (app-server is the name given to the second check_container invocation)
verifier.py:160 (app-server check_container call validates the running image string contains 'httpd')
verifier.py:160 (app-server check_container call lists ['frontend', 'backend'] as expected)
verifier.py:160 (app-server check_container call passes an empty forbidden list, so 'bridge' is not enforced)
verifier.py:166 (data-store is the name used for the third check_container invocation)
verifier.py:166 (data-store check_container call confirms the running image string contains 'redis')
verifier.py:166 (data-store check_container invocation sets ['backend'] as the expected networks)
verifier.py:166 (data-store check_container uses ['frontend'] as the only forbidden network, so 'bridge' is not enforced)

privileged_info_summary: The verifier requires two bridge networks named 'frontend' and 'backend' and checks that web-proxy (nginx), app-server (httpd), and data-store (redis) are connected to the specified networks with web-proxy isolated from backend and data-store isolated from frontend; Docker Desktop setup pulls nginx:alpine, httpd:alpine, and redis:alpine ahead of time.
